PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Jun-29-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Amanda Ebey for (Petitioner) Carmel Kolak.

Ms. Ebey reported that she has substituted in as counsel for Ms. Kolak, that they have a $10,000.00 bond, that they have a proposed order and that this estate can be wrapped up quickly. The Petition for Letters of Administration was granted (subject to review of the order).

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Allen. (Not Reported)

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Apr-27-2015 Continued To Jun-01-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Carmel Kolak in Propria Persona.

Ms. Kolak reported that she consulted with an attorney and was advised that if this is the course she wished to take that it was not unreasonable and that she has contacted a surety company and that they will supply the bond once she is appointed. The Court stated that there has been an objection by Bernard and that he needs time to file a written objection. Ms. Kolak stated she was not aware of the objection but would speak with Bernard. The Court stated that it will continue the petition to give time for a withdrawal of objection to be filed by Bernard as well as proof of bond. The petition for letters of administration was continued to June 1, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Allen. (Not Reported)

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Apr-20-2015 Continued To Apr-27-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Mar-09-2015 Continued To Apr-20-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Carmel Kolak in Propria Persona.

Ms. Kolak stated that there have been no objections, that there are 5 additional siblings, that she and the decedent lived together and were very close, believes that the other siblings are in agreement to waive bond and that she has not retained counsel due to cost and that Mr. Brady did not own much. The petition for letters of administration was continued to April 20, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for Ms. Kolak to consult with counsel. Subsequent to the hearing Ms. Kolak returned and stated that she had previously met with counsel (Ruth Sullivan)and had been advised to file this petition. The court stated that a bond in the amount of $10,000.00 would need to be filed, or alternatively, waivers of bond from all siblings would need to be filed and suggested speaking with someone to determine if a 13100 would be a better path.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Allen. (Not Reported)
